meta.title: Performance & Test Methodology
meta.description: How Taypro defines, tests, and reports 99%+ dust-removal claims, soiling-related generation recovery, and platform-specific performance for solar cleaning robots in India.

hero.title: How Taypro defines, tests, and reports performance
hero.subtitle: Qualified claims for 99%+ dust removal, generation recovery, fleet impact figures, and platform-specific cleaning metrics.
hero.bodyBeforeLink: Written for asset owners, EPC teams, and O&M leads evaluating robotic dry cleaning at utility scale in India. See
hero.bodyAfterLink: for how robots execute each cycle, then scroll for definitions, test methods, and public-stat assumptions.

statsStrip.heading: Fleet metrics documented on this page

purpose.heading: Purpose of this page
purpose.body: This page explains how Taypro Private Limited defines, measures, and communicates performance figures on taypro.in, including "99%+" dust-removal statements and references to generation or performance ratio recovery. It is intended for asset owners, EPC teams, and O&M leads evaluating robotic dry cleaning for utility-scale PV in India.

principles.heading: Three principles behind every public claim
principles.intro: Marketing numbers on taypro.in are never standalone slogans. Each ties back to a defined measurement, a platform context, and a scope boundary.

item0.title: Per pass or per cycle — not nameplate efficiency
item0.body: Dust-removal figures describe loose particulate removed from module glass in one completed pass or automated cycle. They do not describe panel conversion efficiency or guaranteed plant-wide MWh uplift.

item1.title: Platform-specific mechanics
item1.body: Brush-based semi-automatic paths (HELYX) and dual-pass autonomous paths (GLYDE, GLYDE-X) use different cleaning mechanics. Metrics are quoted per pass or per cycle accordingly — never blended into one universal number.

item2.title: Site assessment for outcomes
item2.body: Generation recovery, water savings, and CO₂ figures depend on your soiling baseline, cleaning cadence, tariff, and weather. Plant-specific diligence requires site data — not homepage aggregates alone.

dustRemoval.heading: What "99%+ dust removal" means
dustRemoval.intro: This is the most common figure on product pages and case studies. Here is the precise definition Taypro uses in technical review.
dustRemoval.means0: Dust removal is the fraction of loose surface particulate removed from the module glass in one completed cleaning pass (HELYX) or automated dual-pass cycle (GLYDE, GLYDE-X), under defined test or field conditions.
dustRemoval.means1: Figures are platform-dependent: brush-based semi-automatic paths and dual-pass autonomous paths use different mechanics and are quoted per pass or per cycle accordingly.
dustRemoval.not0: It does not mean the solar panel converts 99% of sunlight to electricity.
dustRemoval.not1: It does not mean the plant will gain 99% more energy output from a single cleaning event.
dustRemoval.not2: It does not guarantee that every module on site reaches the same dust-removal result in every weather or dust-load condition.

generation.heading: Generation recovery vs. module efficiency
generation.p0: Soiling on Indian utility plants can suppress usable output when washing is infrequent. Industry discussions often cite roughly 8–25% seasonal soiling loss depending on region, tilt, and O&M practice. Robotic dry cleaning aims to recover energy that would otherwise be lost to dust — not to raise nameplate module efficiency.
generation.p1Before: Actual MWh recovered depends on your baseline soiling, cleaning cadence, tariff, and weather. Taypro does not publish a single universal "efficiency increase %" for all sites. Use the
generation.p1After: for directional economics, then request a plant-specific assessment from our applications team.

testing.heading: How we test and validate
testing.intro: Taypro validates cleaning performance through a combination of controlled trials, deployed fleet evidence, and third-party certification — not a single lab snapshot.

step0.title: Bench & field trials
step0.body: Controlled trials on representative module types, using standardised artificial and natural dust loads where applicable.

step1.title: Visual & gravimetric checks
step1.body: Before/after surface condition and mass of removed dust on deployed platforms in live field conditions.

step2.title: Fleet operational data
step2.body: Cycle completion, route coverage, and performance ratio trends where customers share SCADA or IV data under NDA.

step3.title: Third-party certification
step3.body: Selected platforms are TÜV NORD certified. Certification scope is stated on product datasheets and customer proposals.

testing.note: Results vary with dust type (agricultural film, sand, pollen, post-storm mix), module tilt, tracker motion, and whether one or two passes are completed in a cycle.

platform.heading: Platform summary
platform.intro: Each Taypro platform is quoted on its own cleaning mechanic. Compare paths below — then review product datasheets for your layout.
platform.glydeMethod: Airflow + microfiber drum; autonomous row run
platform.helyxMethod: Counter-rotating PBT brushes; operator placement per row
platform.glydeXMetric: 99%+ dust per dual-pass cycle on tracker tables
platform.glydeXMethod: Dual-pass dry cleaning with flexible bridge; tracker compatibility per site assessment

uptime.heading: Uptime and service commitments
uptime.body: Marketing references to "highest uptime" or "same-day breakdown" response describe Taypro service targets backed by pan-India spares, NECTYR remote diagnostics, and AMC programs. Contractual SLAs are defined in customer agreements — not on this page.

publicProof.heading: Fleet impact figures on taypro.in
publicProof.intro: Hero, company, and product pages cite aggregate fleet figures. Each uses approved public marketing assumptions for India utility-scale deployments. Rounded tiles may differ slightly from plant-specific models.

item0.label: 5 GW+ solar assets cleaned daily
item0.body: Daily operational throughput across the live robotic cleaning fleet — capacity under active cleaning programmes, not nameplate DC installed industry-wide.

item1.label: 11 billion+ panels cleaned annually
item1.body: Aggregates completed cleaning passes across deployed automatic and semi-automatic fleets (11,00,00,00,000 panel passes per year, fleet aggregate).

item2.label: 150+ live sites
item2.body: Operational deployments with active Taypro cleaning programmes and fleet telemetry on NECTYR where applicable.

item3.label: 188 GWh+ additional clean generation
item3.body: Compares waterless robotic cleaning cadence to typical wet-wash baselines — recovered output from reduced soiling, not nameplate module efficiency gains.

item4.label: 700M+ litres of water saved annually
item4.body: Uses CEEW-class wet-wash benchmarks (~7,000 L per MW per cycle × ~20 cycles per year vs. waterless robotic cleaning across the fleet model).

item5.label: 93k+ metric tons of CO₂ avoided
item5.body: Directional ESG model output from generation recovery assumptions and grid-emission factors. Plant-specific outcomes require site assessment and local tariff validation.

publicProof.footnote: Directional ESG model outputs may differ slightly from rounded public tiles. Validate reported water, generation, and carbon statistics with local SCADA and tariffs before lender or board packs.

documentation.heading: Plant-specific documentation
documentation.bodyBefore: For tenders, lender diligence, or internal technical review, Taypro can provide site-specific cleaning plans, cycle assumptions, and — where available — anonymised field outcomes from comparable deployments.
documentation.bodyAfter: with plant capacity, layout, and location.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for procurement and technical diligence teams.
faq.q0: Does 99%+ dust removal mean my plant will generate 99% more energy?
faq.a0: No. It describes loose surface dust removed from module glass in one completed pass or dual-pass cycle under defined conditions — not a guaranteed MWh uplift percentage for your entire plant.
faq.q1: How often should robots clean a utility-scale plant?
faq.a1: Cadence depends on soiling rate, season, and O&M policy. Many Indian utility programmes run roughly 3–10 dry cycles per month on automatic fleets, weather permitting — not daily plant-wide washing of every module.
faq.q2: Are Rajasthan sand loads and agricultural film soiling treated the same?
faq.a2: No. Dust type, module tilt, tracker motion, and whether one or two passes complete in a cycle all affect outcomes. Field trials and gravimetric checks account for these differences; universal guarantees do not.
faq.q3: How are homepage fleet stats like 5 GW+ and 11B+ panels calculated?
faq.a3: They use approved public marketing assumptions aggregated across live deployments — daily throughput, completed panel passes, and fleet-scale models. They are not substitutes for plant-specific SCADA validation.
faq.q4: What is the difference between GLYDE and HELYX performance quotes?
faq.a4: GLYDE and GLYDE-X quote 99%+ dust removal per automated dual-pass cycle (airflow + microfiber). NYUMA and NYUMA-X quote 98%+ per single-pass PBT cycle. HELYX quotes 95%+ per single forward brush pass with operator placement per row.
faq.q5: Can Taypro share data for tender or lender diligence?
faq.a5: Yes. Contact our applications team with plant capacity, layout, and location. We can provide site-specific cleaning plans, cycle assumptions, and anonymised comparable outcomes where available.
